compiled_state.title: Frictionless Worktree-to-Session Merge with Gate-Verified Attestation
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context

When the sprint orchestrator merges sub-agent worktree branches into the session branch, the pre-commit test gate and review gate block the merge commit because the diff hash changes post-merge. The sub-agent already passed both gates in its own worktree — the code is byte-identical in a clean merge — but the session branch's artifacts directory has no test-gate-status or review-status matching the post-merge hash. This forces the orchestrator into 3-4 manual retry cycles per merge (re-record test status, clear review artifacts, write new findings, re-record review, retry commit). In a 4-layer sprint, this consumed ~20 extra tool calls and significant orchestrator context, and introduced multiple opportunities for error.

## Success Criteria

- When the orchestrator runs harvest-worktree.sh <worktree-branch>, a clean worktree merge completes in a single invocation — no retry cycles, no manual re-recording of test or review status
- When a worktree branch's test-gate-status or review-status is missing or failed, harvest-worktree.sh refuses to merge and exits non-zero with a diagnostic message
- When the merge produces conflicts in files other than .test-index, harvest-worktree.sh aborts the merge and exits non-zero — conflicted merges never receive attestation
- When two worktree branches both add entries to .test-index, the merge resolves automatically without manual conflict resolution (union merge driver)
- When harvest-worktree.sh attests test/review status, the attestation includes the source worktree ID so the trust chain is auditable via the status file
- The pre-commit test gate and review gate hooks are not modified — gate integrity is preserved by the existing validation logic, not weakened by new bypass paths

## Scenario Analysis

Critical:
- Diff hash divergence between attestation and pre-commit verification (runtime): harvest computes hash then stages .test-index, changing the index. Mitigation: compute hash AFTER all staging.
- record-review.sh overlap check fails during attestation (runtime): overlap check rejects worktree findings against merge diff. Mitigation: add --attest mode or write review-status with documented format contract.

High:
- compute-diff-hash.sh cache invalidation race (runtime): HFS+ 1s mtime resolution causes stale cache. Mitigation: compute hash once after all staging.
- Session-side auto-merged changes in shared files (runtime): standard git merge behavior — both sides independently reviewed. No attestation gap.
- MERGE_HEAD persists after failed harvest (runtime): Mitigation: cleanup via trap on failure.
- No API for attest-without-re-running (runtime): Mitigation: add --attest flag to recording scripts or documented format contract.

Medium:
- Union driver duplicate .test-index entries (runtime): cosmetic accumulation.
- Worktree artifacts purged by OS temp cleaner (configuration): low probability edge case.
- Concurrent harvest calls (runtime): serialized by orchestrator protocol; validate no MERGE_HEAD on entry.
- Checkpoint-aware diff base divergence (runtime): handle in script implementation.

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Two components: (1) a .test-index merge=union entry in .gitattributes to auto-resolve the chronic append-only conflict, and (2) a harvest-worktree.sh script that verifies the worktree agent's gate artifacts, merges with --no-commit, re-records test-gate-status and review-status with the post-merge diff hash (attestation for clean merges only), then commits. The per-worktree-review-commit.md workflow is updated to use the script. Conflicted merges (beyond .test-index) are rejected — the orchestrator must resolve and run full gates.
